name: Bagdad Theater
description: McMenamins restored 1920s movie palace, pub and theater

name: Forest Park
description: One of the country's largest urban forest reserves, over 80 miles of trails

name: Mill Ends Park
description: World's smallest park, a two-foot circle in a median on Naito Parkway

name: OMSI
description: Oregon Museum of Science and Industry, submarine USS Blueback docked outside

name: Pioneer Courthouse Square
description: Known as Portland's living room, public square, brick amphitheater, food carts

name: Pittock Mansion
description: French Renaissance-style château, panoramic views of the city and Mt. Hood

name: Portland Art Museum
description: Oldest art museum in the Pacific Northwest, Native American art collection

name: Powell's City of Books
description: World's largest independent bookstore, occupies a full city block, color-coded rooms

name: St. Johns Bridge
description: Gothic steel suspension bridge over the Willamette, Cathedral Park underneath

name: Tom McCall Waterfront Park
description: Riverfront park, cherry blossoms in spring, site of many festivals, runs along the Willamette River

name: Washington Park
description: International Rose Test Garden, Japanese Garden, Oregon Zoo, Hoyt Arboretum
